message:
test(regression): pin issue #76 IN subquery + IN predicate AND-combination

Adds a guarding regression test for the recently-fixed issue #76:
combining `id IN (SELECT ...)` with another `IN (?, ?)` predicate via
AND returned an empty result set when both predicates bound through
parameters, even though each predicate individually matched rows.

The fix landed already; this test pins the property so a future
optimizer or planner change can't silently re-introduce the empty-set
behavior. The test:

- Builds a small `issues` + `labels` schema with two rows and shared
  `core` labels (mirroring the original beads_rust workload that
  surfaced the bug).
- Exercises four shapes:
  * `IN (SELECT ...)` alone
  * `IN (?)` alone
  * the AND-combination of both — the historically broken case
  * the same AND-combination as a prepared statement with parameter
    rebinding, since that's the path beads_rust hits in practice.

All four expect the `core`-labelled, open issue ids back. Uses
`fsqlite_types::SqliteValue::Text` extraction directly (no helper
crate) so the test stays loadable from the workspace without extra
dev-dependencies.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
